task.title: Add changelog-update helper tied to docs-impact results.

context.problem_statement: Release notes are part of documentation drift. The kit already detects docs-impact and has local versioning commands, but operators need a deterministic helper that turns docs-impact/versioning context into a reviewable changelog proposal or check rather than relying on ad hoc agent memory.
background: AGW-022 and AGW-023 made docs-impact and changelog-update part of the PR interface contract, but /update-changelog is still spec-only.
The versioning profile creates target-owned VERSION, CHANGELOG.md, and docs/versioning.md plus make version-check/version-bump.
AGW-021 is closed in source commit 6417391 and kit commit 3fdbbcd; both remotes match local HEADs at packet creation.
The kit checkout is clean at repo-contract-kit 0.4.49, and make version-check && make docs-check passed at packet creation.
non_goals: Do not create an autonomous release bot or open PRs.
Do not write CHANGELOG.md by default.
Do not change SemVer bump semantics or release tagging.
Do not replace docs-impact, docs-propose, version-check, or version-bump.
Do not edit agent-workflow-kit prompt/schema source except for source backlog closeout after kit validation passes.

report_sources: git ls-remote origin refs/heads/main for source and kit remotes
finalizer_receipt_paths: source main commit 6417391e36f1c485f6f90c152754c0abe2fc738c closed AGW-021; source agent-task-status reported no active, stale, hazardous, or unknown-scope tasks
previous_task_state.notes: AGW-024 packet creation began only after AGW-021 was pushed in both repos, both remotes matched local HEADs, both worktrees were clean, and source task-status reported zero active tasks.

closeout_required_before_start.reason: The previous AGW-021 task has source and kit closeout commits, matching remote refs, clean worktrees, and no active task metadata.
closeout_required_before_start.required_next_step: Proceed with exactly one AGW-024 worker in repo-contract-kit; refresh task-status if source or kit state becomes dirty before worker launch.

goal_alignment.repo_goal: Keep repo-contract-kit as the install-layer guardrail provider that helps local agents detect documentation/release-note impact and produce reviewable evidence without mutating target repos by default.

area_contracts.purpose: Classifies changed paths into docs-impact categories and provides the source signal for release-note/changelog impact.
Validates VERSION and performs explicit version bumps with changelog stubs.
Stable wrapper CLI for any changelog proposal/check command.
Installed Make target surface for local changelog proposal/check workflows.
Spec-only /update-changelog grammar that should remain consistent with the new local helper.

stop_conditions: Stop if the helper would write CHANGELOG.md or VERSION by default.
Stop if the helper duplicates or weakens docs-impact/version-check gates.
Stop if the helper cannot produce deterministic output suitable for tests.

protected_files: Target repo CHANGELOG.md and VERSION must remain target-owned and not overwritten by kit updates.
Spec-only PR command grammar must not imply hosted bot execution unless implemented.
expected_outputs: A deterministic changelog proposal/check helper that can consume docs-impact output or derive it from changed files/working tree.
JSON and text output that reports docs-impact categories, release-note/changelog need, candidate changelog text, target VERSION/CHANGELOG state, and next safe commands.
Default behavior is non-mutating; any write/update behavior, if implemented, is explicit and preserves versioning profile ownership rules.
Wrapper CLI and installed Make target expose the helper.
Docs explain how the helper relates to docs-impact, version-check, version-bump, and /update-changelog.
VERSION and CHANGELOG.md record the repo-contract-kit behavior change.

acceptance_criteria.description: A local changelog-update helper produces deterministic JSON/text proposals from docs-impact or changed-file input without writing by default.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused helper tests cover docs-impacting and no-impact changes and assert no target writes.

acceptance_criteria.description: The helper reports target VERSION/CHANGELOG presence, whether a changelog update appears required, a candidate changelog entry, and next safe commands such as version-check or version-bump.
acceptance_criteria.verification: Focused helper tests inspect JSON fields and text output.

acceptance_criteria.description: Wrapper CLI and installed Make target expose the helper while preserving non-mutating defaults.
Docs and slash-command grammar explain the local helper and keep write behavior behind explicit accepted scope.
The behavior change is versioned and documented in repo-contract-kit.
Source backlog rows and summary close AGW-024 only after kit validation passes.

commands.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 python3 -m unittest tests.test_changelog_update tests.test_repo_contract_kit_cli tests.test_install tests.test_versioning

commands.expected_result: pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout
pass in source repo after closeout

readiness_check.expected_result: pass before lifecycle finish, or blocked with explicit receipt if validation cannot complete

lifecycle_action.expected_result: done metadata or explicit blocked finalizer receipt

final_task_status.expected_result: no active AGW-024 task metadata after parent closeout, or terminal metadata with linked receipt before cleanup

closeout_preview.expected_result: preview reports no unsafe dirty terminal worktree removal

closeout_requirements.dirty_state_explanation: Worker must explain any remaining dirty files in the kit checkout; parent should only integrate expected AGW-024 files and leave both repos clean after commits.

docs_impact.notes: This adds an operator-facing command and release-note workflow, so docs and changelog/version updates are required.

known_risks: A changelog helper could imply release authority if docs are unclear.
A write mode could accidentally modify target-owned CHANGELOG.md or VERSION.
Docs-impact categories may be too broad for a useful release note without clear caveats.
stop_conditions: Any default mode writes CHANGELOG.md, VERSION, or target files.
The helper bypasses docs-impact or version-check rather than composing them.
The helper cannot produce deterministic testable output.

approval.notes: User requested implementing all remaining open backlog items with one task agent per item and a committed packet before each task.

handoff.next_packet_hint: After AGW-024, make agent-next should select AGW-025 unless priority changes.
